notes: Comprehensive discovery with strong out-of-scope discipline and constraint coverage, but D1 is partially solution-framed (section 1 describes the proposed feature architecture before completing problem framing), D2 lacks per-persona cost/timing specificity, D3's MVP section (section 23) lists guardrails rather than a crisp bounded scope with explicit deferrals, D5 assumptions are mostly present but some are low-uncertainty technical statements rather than genuine business/regulatory uncertainties with stated risk-if-wrong, and D6 references the 340-incident baseline but lacks an explicit numerical target reduction.
